question: Is Al Fardan Q9 a real company? How can I verify?
answer: Yes — Al-Fardan Q9 is the digital-asset arm of the Al-Fardan Group. Ways to verify us: (1) DIFC Public Register — we're registered at DIFC with registration #5605, searchable at difc.ae/public-register. (2) VARA Public Register — our VARA-licensed infrastructure partner Fuze (Morpheus Software Technology FZE) holds Licence VL/23/10/002 issued 17 November 2023; listed at vara.ae/en/licenses-and-register/public-register/morpheus-software-technology-fze-fuze/. (3) MoU with Fuze signed 10 December 2023 — announced publicly in Gulf News. (4) Al-Fardan Exchange (parent heritage brand) has been CBUAE-regulated since 1971. (5) Physical office at The Onyx Tower 2, The Greens, Sheikh Zayed Road, Dubai — open for in-person visits by appointment. (6) SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27017 + ISO 9001 certificates, independently audited annually. Would you like me to send all public-register links?
aliases: how do I verify Al Fardan
how do I know you're legit

question: What licenses do you have and from which regulators?
answer: Here's the complete picture: (1) VARA — regulated digital-asset activity runs through our VARA-licensed infrastructure partner Fuze (Morpheus Software Technology FZE), VARA Licence VL/23/10/002 issued 17 November 2023. (2) DIFC — Al-Fardan Q9 is directly DIFC-registered with registration #5605. (3) CBUAE — the parent Al-Fardan Exchange has been CBUAE-regulated since 1971. (4) We do NOT directly hold a DFSA licence — DFSA is a separate financial-services licence, and we operate under the VARA framework via Fuze instead. Compliance certifications (all independently audited annually): SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 (information security) + ISO 27017 (cloud security) + ISO 9001 (quality management). MoU with Fuze signed 10 December 2023. Would you like me to send the licence + audit documentation?

question: Where are my assets held? Who is the custodian?
answer: Your assets are held in segregated Fireblocks MPC-CMP vaults — one dedicated vault per client, legally ring-fenced from our corporate balance sheet and never commingled with other clients' assets or our own funds. Fireblocks is the institutional-grade custody infrastructure used by top global banks and exchanges; private keys are split across multiple Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) so no single party can unilaterally move funds. Coverage on top of custody: $250M Lloyd's of London policy (SY-2025-49881) against theft, hacks, and insider collusion. In case of our insolvency, your assets are returnable to you — they are NOT part of our bankruptcy estate. Would you like our proof-of-reserves report or the custody legal structure documentation?

question: What's the minimum investment amount?
answer: We have three tiers, pick the one that fits you: (1) Starter Access — minimum USD 3,000 — aimed at clients building toward our standard minimum, full custody + retail OTC access, light-touch KYC. (2) Standard Individual — minimum USD 10,000 — full access to Custody + Staking + Lending + retail OTC. (3) Institutional / Family Office — no fixed minimum, custom onboarding with entity KYC and dedicated relationship manager; typical clients start at USD 100K+ but we set limits by mandate. Which tier fits your situation?

question: Which countries are restricted from using Al Fardan Q9?
answer: Al-Fardan Q9 welcomes investors from around the world — our platform is NOT restricted to UAE residents. The only exclusion: clients resident in OFAC-sanctioned jurisdictions (currently North Korea, Iran, Syria, Crimea, and a small number of specifically designated regions) plus anyone on the UN / EU / UK / UAE consolidated sanctions lists. Everywhere else — UAE, wider GCC, Europe, Asia, Africa, Americas — is supported subject to passing our standard KYC + AML review. Would you like me to check whether your country is eligible?

question: What support do I get, and do I get a dedicated account manager?
answer: Support is tiered by relationship size: (1) Starter / Standard accounts — 24/7 support via in-app chat, email (support@alfardanq9.com), and WhatsApp; typical response under 2 UAE business hours. (2) Clients with USD 1M+ AUM receive a dedicated private relationship manager with direct mobile/Signal access and priority routing into compliance, OTC, and lending teams. (3) Institutional mandates — senior relationship manager plus scheduled weekly or fortnightly calls as agreed at onboarding, plus bespoke reporting. Does your portfolio qualify for the dedicated-RM tier?

question: Do you have regulatory audits? Who audits you?
answer: Yes — several ongoing audit regimes: (1) Annual compliance audit covering VARA-infrastructure alignment (via our partner Fuze, Licence VL/23/10/002) and DIFC conduct-of-business rules under our DIFC registration #5605. (2) Annual SOC 2 Type II re-certification by independent assessors. (3) Annual ISO 27001 (infosec) + ISO 27017 (cloud) + ISO 9001 (quality) re-certification. (4) Financial-statement audit by our external auditor, available under NDA for qualified institutional clients. Note: we do NOT hold a DFSA licence directly — our regulated activity sits under the VARA framework via Fuze. Would you like me to share the audit certificates under NDA?

question: Do you get third-party security audits?
answer: Yes — four independent third parties touch our stack: (1) Deloitte — quarterly Proof-of-Reserves attestation + financial-statement audit. (2) SOC 2 Type II — annual independent assessor report (controls covering security, availability, confidentiality). (3) ISO 27001 (information security management) + ISO 27017 (cloud security) — annual independent certification. (4) Fireblocks — our custody infrastructure is itself SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + PCI-DSS Level 1 certified, audited independently by top-tier firms. Certificates available on request under standard NDA. Want me to share the certificate pack?

question: What are the account tiers and what does each include?
answer: Three tiers: STARTER ACCESS (≥ USD 3K) — full custody, retail OTC access, in-app + email chat support, Shariah-compliant by default, no monthly fees. STANDARD INDIVIDUAL (≥ USD 10K) — everything in Starter plus full staking on ETH / SOL / DOT / ADA / AVAX / ATOM / POL, lending against BTC / ETH collateral, priority support routing, quarterly portfolio review email. INSTITUTIONAL / FAMILY OFFICE (custom, typical USD 100K+) — everything in Standard plus dedicated relationship manager ($1M+), bespoke OTC spreads, weekly strategy calls, custom reporting, direct settlement in 8 currencies, and discretionary-mandate availability. Which tier fits your situation?
aliases: what do I get at each tier

question: Which crypto assets are supported on each service?
answer: CUSTODY: BTC, ETH, SOL, USDT, USDC, and major stablecoins and large-caps. STAKING: ETH (~5.2% APY), SOL (~7.1%), POL (~5.8%), ADA (~4.5%), DOT (~14.2%), AVAX (~8.5%), ATOM (~18.5%) — Bitcoin is proof-of-work so cannot be staked. LENDING (collateral you pledge): BTC, ETH. LENDING (currency you receive): USD, AED, EUR via SWIFT to your registered IBAN. OTC DESK: 16+ digital assets including BTC, ETH, SOL, USDT, USDC, BNB, XRP, ADA, AVAX, DOT, MATIC/POL, LINK, ATOM, UNI, AAVE, ARB plus GCC-currency settlement in 8 currencies. Which asset or service interests you most?

question: Do you offer a referral program or commissions?
answer: Not at this time. We don't currently run a formal referral or commission programme. We may introduce one in the future — if and when we do, it'll be announced formally through the platform and via email. For now: if you refer a qualified institutional client who onboards with us, please reach out to your relationship manager and we can acknowledge it on a case-by-case basis (typically fee credits on your own account rather than cash commission). Any questions about introducing a specific client?

question: Is the entire platform Sharia-compliant?
answer: Yes — our ENTIRE platform is Shariah-compliant, across all four services: Custody (1:1 asset-backed, no re-hypothecation), Staking (rewards are compensation for network-security services, not interest / riba), OTC (spot settlement, no derivative gharar), and Lending (Murabaha credit lines — disclosed cost, not interest). Supervision: independent Shariah Supervisory Board chaired by Sheikh Dr. Tariq Al-Mahrouqi, a member of the AAOIFI Shariah Board. Our products avoid riba (interest), gharar (excessive uncertainty), and maysir (speculation). Full quarterly Shariah reports are published on our Shariah Reports page. Would you like the latest Shariah compliance certificate?

question: What happens to my assets if Al Fardan Q9 goes bankrupt?
answer: Your assets are returnable to you — they are NOT part of our bankruptcy estate. Because custody is structured with segregated Fireblocks MPC vaults legally ring-fenced from our corporate balance sheet, client assets do not enter the insolvency pool. In the scenario: (1) A court-appointed trustee reviews the estate. (2) The trustee transfers the Fireblocks policy-signing rights to a successor custodian. (3) You continue to access your assets through the successor with the same keys-under-MPC structure. Your Lloyd's $250M insurance policy remains in force through the transition. This is specifically the structure we built to prevent any FTX-style commingled-funds failure. Would you like a walk-through of the legal ring-fencing documentation?
aliases: do I get my assets back

question: Are there any lock-up periods on my funds?
answer: No lock-up periods on our side. You can deposit, withdraw, and rebalance at any time during normal market hours. The only wait times are protocol-level unbonding periods for staked assets — these are set by the blockchain itself, not by us: ETH ~27 hours to unstake, SOL ~2 days, POL no lock-up, ADA no lock-up, DOT 28 days (network rule), AVAX 14 days (network rule), ATOM 21 days (network rule). Custody balances and lending collateral have zero lock-up beyond what the network enforces. Would you like me to explain unbonding for a specific asset?
